data.content: The project uses **MSTest v3** with the Microsoft Testing Platform enabled. Tests are categorized via `[TestCategory]`. I'll filter for the "Integration" category.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with category filter

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with category filter

data.reasoningText:
The project uses **MSTest** (v3.8.0) with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is set to `true` in Directory.Build.props). The tests are categorized with `TestCategory` - "Unit" and "Integration". The user wants to run only the Integration tests.

With MSTest v3 and the Microsoft Testing Platform, filtering by category uses `--filter` with `TestCategory=Integration`.

arguments.description: Run integration tests with category filter

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-59fe865aeb23445e9646c0cba430465b && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with category filter", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run integration tests with category filter

data.content:
All done! The project uses **MSTest v3.8.0** with the Microsoft Testing Platform. The integration test (`GetAll_QueriesDatabase`) passed successfully using:

```
dotnet test --filter "TestCategory=Integration"
```
